title: Iran Conflict and the Strait of Hormuz: Impacts on Oil, Gas, and Other Commodities
summary: CRS report documenting US-Israeli military operations against Iran since February 2026, oil price history including Jun 2025 +8.5% spike, and Strait of Hormuz closure mechanics as of March 2026.

title: Iran war threatens prolonged impact on energy markets as oil prices rise
summary: Reports ~20% of global crude/LNG supply suspended, ~140M barrels frozen in Gulf ports, oil prices surged >25% since war start, and political vulnerability for Trump ahead of midterms.

title: How Iran Blocking the Strait of Hormuz Affects the U.S.
summary: Rates Trump's claim that Strait closure 'doesn't really affect' the US as misleading; documents 50-cent+ gasoline spike and IEA data on Strait traffic slowing to a trickle.

title: How attack on Iran could impact global oil market and economy
summary: Kpler data: >14M bbl/day through Strait in 2025 (⅓ of seaborne exports); expert Robert McNally: 'A prolonged closure is a guaranteed global recession'; Brent at $72.48 day before conflict.

title: The US-Iran War: The potential economic impact and how to respond
summary: Describes conflict as largest US military operation since 2003 Iraq invasion; Brent surged 13% intraday to $83/bbl, WTI to $76; major oil companies suspended Strait shipments.

title: How the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran will test the stock market, economy
summary: Documents crude oil +35% in first week post-Strait closure; S&P 500 –2%, Europe –7%, EM –8.4% in first 5 trading days; energy sector is only 3.4% of S&P 500.

title: S&P 500 Index Forecast: Iran Conflict, CPI, Fed
summary: S&P 500 at 6,683 on Mar 12 (–2.5% YTD); RSI 35.9 (near oversold); Feb CPI 2.4% YoY; FOMC rate-cut probability 4.7%; Fed cuts pushed to September 2026.

title: Monthly Market Memo – February 2026: U.S.-Israel Strike on Iran
summary: S&P 500 –0.75% in Feb; small/mid-caps +2.5%, intl equities +5%, bonds +1.5%; documents major 2026 rotation away from US large-cap growth predating and amplified by Iran conflict.

title: What does the US-Israel attack on Iran mean for oil prices?
summary: Reports first Iranian Strait closure since US military threats; second live-fire drill in Strait in recent weeks; Spain condemns US-Israeli strikes.

title: Oil prices surge 13% in first trades after start of US-Iran conflict
summary: Brent hit $82.37 intraday on Day 1 (+13% from prior close of ~$72.80); confirmed 30% YTD gain from Jan 1 2026 baseline of ~$61/bbl.

title: Oil prices sink 13% as Trump predicts Middle East de-escalation
summary: Brent fell 12.6% to $86.50/bbl on March 10 after Trump said war was 'very complete'; both benchmarks had hit $119/bbl on March 9.

title: Two pipelines helping Saudi Arabia, UAE bypass the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Saudi Petroline capacity up to 7 mbd ramping toward full; UAE ADCOP at 71% utilization with ~440k bpd spare; Ruwais refinery fire limits UAE upside.

title: Energy Supplies Under a Prolonged Middle East Conflict
summary: EIA estimates 2.6 mbd bypass capacity; IEA estimates 4.2 mbd; Hormuz normally carries 20 mbd; OPEC+ agreed +206,000 bpd on March 1.

title: 2026 Hormuz Strait Disruption: Oil Market Impacts and Supply Risks
summary: Brent rose from $73 (late Feb) to $103 by mid-March; pipeline bypass + spare capacity covers less than half the gap; $110-130/bbl forecast if disruption persists.

title: Strait of Hormuz Closure 2026: What It Means for Asian Oil Markets
summary: OPEC+ nominal spare of 4.1 mbd mostly held by Saudi/UAE; actual agreed increase on March 1 was only 206,000 bpd; Qatar halted all LNG after Ras Laffan drone strike.

title: Analysts hike oil price outlooks as Iran conflict enters third week
summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader vowed to keep Hormuz shut; Brent and WTI headed for 10%+ weekly gains in week 3; analysts raised price targets toward $100+ and $120+ scenarios.

title: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve
summary: The US SPR current level is approximately 388 million barrels.

title: OPEC+ Production Strategy
summary: Saudi Arabia has decided to increase OPEC+ production at a slower pace.

title: Ruwais Refinery Fire
summary: The Ruwais refinery fire has been largely contained.

title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz Crisis
publisher: Wikipedia (citing NYT, Guardian, Reuters, Al Jazeera)
summary: Comprehensive event timeline: Feb 28 strikes, Mar 4 Hormuz declared closed, Iran mine-laying, war-risk insurance premiums surging.

title: Oil prices fall and stocks rebound after Trump says Iran war could end 'very soon'
summary: Confirms Brent intraday peak $119.50 on Mar 9, drop on Trump 'very soon' post; oil still ~25% above pre-war levels after drop.

title: Oil jumps to 2022 high on Iran war, falls after close as Russia sanctions in doubt
summary: Brent settles at 2022 high Mar 8; post-settlement reversal on Trump-Putin call and Russia sanctions easing rumors; both benchmarks +35% from war start.

title: What higher European gas prices mean for BESS revenues and investments
summary: Confirms Ras Laffan shutdown Mar 2 via Iranian drone strikes; TTF surged ~70% in 2 sessions to €53/MWh close, €60+ intraday; GS estimates €74/MWh (1-month) and >€100/MWh (2-month) scenarios.

title: Qatar's LNG Shutdown Sparks 50% Price Spike — Ending 2026 Buyer's Market
summary: QatarEnergy attack halts 10.2 Bcf/d LNG supply; TTF +50%, JKM +39%; world's largest LNG export plant offline 5+ days — longest since 2008.

title: How Will the Iran Conflict Impact Oil Prices?
summary: Goldman Sachs estimates TTF could reach €74/MWh if Hormuz closed 1 month; >€100/MWh if 2+ months; 20% of global oil and LNG transits the strait.

title: Oil prices fallout: OPEC producers boost output after Iran strikes
summary: OPEC+ boosts output 206k bpd post-Iran strikes; IEA monitoring; US gas price at $3/gal pre-war, SPR release under consideration.

title: Qatar LNG Shutdown: Global Gas Prices Soaring — EnvironmentEnergyLeader
summary: India LNG prices rise to $23.3/MMBtu (+$7.80 in one session); at least 8 LNG tankers diverted to Asia from Europe; Petronet LNG force majeure confirmed.

title: yfinance market data: BZ=F, CL=F, GC=F, ^GSPC, ^VIX, TLT
summary: Live market data pulled for Oct 2024–Jun 2025 baseline; current Brent $67.61, WTI $65.11, Gold $3,294, S&P 500 6,204, VIX 16.73.

title: US to Release 172 Million Barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright announces 172Mb SPR release as part of IEA 400Mb coordinated emergency response to Iran war oil shock.

title: Planned SPR Release Would Put US Supplies at Lowest in 44 Years
summary: SPR post-release level ~243Mb would be the lowest since 1982; gas prices up 21.8% from $2.98 to $3.63/gal by mid-March.

title: Crude Oil Prices Swing Wildly as Iran War Stretches On
summary: Brent surged from $70 pre-attack to $93 by end of first week; gas prices jumped $0.50/gal to $3.45; diesel up ~90 cents in one week.

title: Oil Price Tops $100 Again as Iran Strikes Economic Targets
summary: Brent peaked at $119.48/bbl on March 11 (up 29% intraday); Deutsche Bank warns of 'stagflationary shock'; IEA orders 400Mb release.

title: The Oil Market Faces New Tensions and Challenges — Goldman Sachs Forecasts
summary: Goldman raised Q4 2026 Brent forecast to $71/bbl; expects Mar-Apr avg $98/bbl; upside scenario (1-month Hormuz closure) → $110/bbl avg; Macquarie sees $150+ risk.

title: IEA Coordinates Largest-Ever Emergency Oil Reserve Release
summary: IEA 32-nation coordinated 400Mb release; Hormuz flow at <10% of pre-war levels; Iran targeting Gulf Arab oil fields/refineries to pressure US/Israel ceasefire.

title: Brent Crude Price Data — yfinance
summary: Live Brent futures (BZ=F): $69.21 as of July 14 2025 (pre-conflict baseline period).

title: J.P. Morgan 2026 Oil Price Forecast
summary: JPM expects targeted military action to avoid Iran oil infra; cites historical 76% average oil price increase from regime-change events; flags stagflation risk.
